zh.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多
zh.Of, Ayala, New Castile, Spain: 西班牙劍橋大學默里 卡斯利
zh.lifeSummary: 當María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya於1158年出生在西班牙時，她的父親VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala30歲，母親Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza28歲。她和Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña至少有1個兒子。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多
zh-hans.Of, Ayala, New Castile, Spain: 西班牙劍橋大學默里 卡斯利
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya于1158年出生在西班牙时，她的父亲VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala30岁，母亲Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza28岁。她和Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña至少有1个儿子。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Salcedo,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Francisco, Rafael, Ana, Jesus, Carlos, Manuel, Miguel, Pedro, Jorge.

Spanish: habitational name from any of numerous places called Salcedo, named with a collective form of salce ‘willow tree’, or a topographic name denoting someone living by willow trees. Compare Saucedo .

variants.name: Salcido
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Jesus, Carlos, Juan, Ruben, Luis, Roberto, Raul, Francisco, Juana, Adelina.
Hispanic (Mexico): probably an altered form of Spanish   Salcedo  .

variants.name: Saucedo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Francisco, Raul, Miguel, Roberto, Alfredo, Guadalupe.
Spanish: variant of   Salcedo  . Compare   Sausedo  .

nameMeanings.name: Señora
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hispanic (Mexico): habitational name from Sonora, the name of a state in Mexico, probably named from Spanish Señora, a form of address of the Virgin Mary.
Galician (Soñora): habitational name from any of the two places called Soñora in A Coruña.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.

nameMeanings.name: Ayala
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Jesus, Miguel, Pedro, Francisco, Raul, Jorge, Rafael.
Basque: Castilianized form of Aiala, habitational or topographic name from ai ‘slope, hillside’ + al(h)a ‘pasture’.

nameMeanings.name: las
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name, nickname, or randomly assigned by Polish clerks, from Polish las ‘forest, wood’.
Jewish (American): shortened form of some original Ashkenazic surname.

nameMeanings.name: Casas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Luis, Carlos, Francisco, Raul, Roberto, Ricardo, Ruben, Armando, Guadalupe.  Portuguese Joao, Ligia, Paulo.
Spanish and Portuguese: plural form of   Casa  , frequent as a habitational and topographic name. Compare   De Casas  .
Catalan: Castilianized form of   Cases  .

variants.name: Casa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Camillo, Ciro, Dante, Domenic, Gaetano, Gennaro, Giovanna, Guido, Luigi, Onofrio.  Spanish Blanca, Ernesto, Carlos, Alicia, Esperanza, Martinez, Miguel, Rafael, Adriana, Alberto, Alvarez, Benito.

Italian, Spanish, Catalan, Portuguese, and southern French: topographic name from casa ‘house’ (from Latin casa ‘hut, cottage, cabin’), perhaps originally denoting the occupier of the most distinguished house in a village.
Italian: from a short form of the personal name Benincasa, an omen or well-wishing name meaning ‘welcome in (our) house’.

variants.name: De Casas
meanings: Spanish and Portuguese: variant of Casas , with the preposition de ‘from’.

nameMeanings.name: Reales
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Spanish: plural form of   Real  .

variants.name: Real
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Guillermo, Jose, Carlos, Francisco, Luis, Ricardo, Alberto, Armando, Blanca, Lourdes, Manuel.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim.

Spanish, Catalan, Portuguese, and Galician: habitational name from any of numerous places called Real; those in Galicia (Spain) and Portugal being named from real ‘royal’ or as variant of Rial , while those in southern Spain and Catalonia are named in part from real meaning ‘encampment, rural property’ (Arabic raḥāl ‘farmhouse, cabin’). Compare Del Real .

Spanish; French (Réal): from Spanish and Old French real ‘royal’, hence a nickname for someone who behaved in a regal manner, or an occupational name for someone in the service of the king, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a land belonging to the king. As a French name it may also be a habitational name from Réal (Pyrénées-Orientales), a place called for having been part of a royal domain (compare Reaux ).

variants.name: Royals
meanings: Perhaps also an Americanized form of Spanish   Reales  .
English (Lancashire): variant of Royal , with post-medieval excrescent -s. Compare Ryals , with which it may have been confused.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Navarra,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Sal, Edgardo, Guido, Santo, Vito, Angelo, Annamarie, Antonio, Benedicto, Camillo, Dante, Emilio, Fausto, Gaetano, Giuseppe, Mario, Romulo, Sergio.  Spanish Agustin, Herlinda, Manuel, Solita.

Spanish, Portuguese, Italian, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from Navarre (Castilian Navarra, Basque Nafarroa, French Navarre), a largely Basque-speaking region now divided between Spain and France, but in the Middle Ages an independent Basque kingdom. The name of the region derives from Basque naba ‘plain next to mountains’. Compare Navarre .

variants.name: Navarrete
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Pedro, Raul, Luis, Miguel, Roberto, Salvador, Francisco.

Spanish: habitational name from any of the places in La Rioja, Aragon, and the Basque Country named in Castilian Navarrete and in Basque Nafarrate (the latter in Araba/Álava province), of Basque origin (compare   Navarra   and   Nafarrate  ).
Basque: Castilianized form of Nafarrate or Nafarrete, cognates of 1.

variants.name: Navarro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Jesus, Luis, Ramon, Francisco, Miguel, Rafael, Jorge, Pedro.
Spanish, Italian, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name for someone from Navarre (see   Navarra  ).

variants.name: Nava
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Carlos, Luis, Pedro, Manuel, Alfredo, Francisco, Ruben, Javier, Enrique.

Spanish, Catalan, and Asturian-Leonese: habitational name from any of numerous places called Nava, named with nava ‘treeless plateau’, a word of pre-Roman origin, most probably from Basque naba ‘plain next to mountains’ (see Navarra ).

variants.name: Navarre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Jacques, Anatole, Angelle, Camille, Marcelle.
French: habitational name from Navarre (see   Navarra  ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Aragón
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Carlos, Juan, Ruben, Fernando, Jesus, Julio, Luis, Raul, Mario, Alfonso.

Spanish (Aragón) and southern French: habitational name from Aragon, Spain, which was an independent kingdom from 1035 to 1479. It took its name from the river Aragón, which arises in its northwestern corner. The river name may be related to Basque (h)ara(n) ‘valley’; in Basque, Aragon is called Aragoa or Aragoi, which probably means ‘high valley’. Compare Deragon .

variants.name: Deragon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Monique, Pierre, Raoul, Rejean.

French: variant of Daragon, habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, denoting someone from Aragon, a region and medieval kingdom in Spain (see Aragon ), or a habitational name for someone from Aragon, the name of several places in various parts of France. The surname Deragon is very rare in France. Compare Dragon .

variants.name: Aragones
meanings: Spanish (Aragonés): habitational or ethnic name for someone from Aragon (see Aragon ).

variants.name: Aragona
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Ciro, Domenic, Domenico, Gaspare.  Spanish Carolina, Ninfa, Rosario.

Italian (mainly southern): habitational or ethnic name for someone from the former kingdom of Aragon (see   Aragon  ); the surname arrived in Italy in the wake of the Aragonese conquest and domination of southern Italy, which began in 1282.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: León
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

Indian: variant of   Dey  .

English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: los
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jozef, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Andrej, Bogdan, Czeslaw, Dorota, Edyta, Iwan, Jerzy, Ryszard, Wladyslaw.
Polish (Łoś): nickname for a large, ungainly person, from Polish łoś ‘elk’.
Dutch: nickname from Middle Dutch losch ‘squinting, cross-eyed’; ‘one-eyed’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vizcaya
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Basque: Castilianized form of Bizkaia, a habitational name from the province of Biscay (Basque Bizkaia, Spanish Vizcaya) in the Basque Country, Spain. The placename Bizkaia derives from Basque bizkai ‘ridge’ + the definite article -a.

variants.name: Vizcaino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Ramon, Jaime, Luis, Raul, Armando, Edmundo, Efrain, Jesus, Jorge.

Spanish (Vizcaíno): habitational name for someone from the province of Biscay (see Vizcaya ) in the Basque Country. Historically, the term was also used to denote a seafarer and in some cases this may be the meaning behind the surname.

nameMeanings.name: María
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Maria
meanings: Short form of   Maria  , of German origin but now also moderately popular in the English-speaking world.

nameMeanings.name: Sáenz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Raul, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Mario, Francisco, Pedro, Ricardo, Alfredo.
Spanish (Sáenz): variant of Sánchez (see Sanchez ).

variants.name: Sainz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Miguel, Manuel, Andres, Francisco, Juan, Luis, Raul, Adriana, Alfredo, Armando.
Spanish (also Sáinz): variant of Saenz .

variants.name: Sanchez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Jorge, Pedro, Ramon, Raul, Roberto.
Spanish (Sánchez): patronymic from the personal name Sancho . In the US, this surname is also found among Native Americans (mainly Pueblos). Compare De Sanchez .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: When María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya was born in 1158, in Spain, her father, VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala, was 30 and her mother, Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza, was 28. She had at least 1 son with Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya est née en 1158, à Espagne, son père, VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza, avait 28 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya 1158 in Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala 30 und ihre Mutter Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza 28. Sie hatte mit Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya è nata il nel 1158 nel Spagna, sua padre, VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza, aveva 28 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España: スペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレド
ja.Of, Ayala, New Castile, Spain: スペインニュー カスティル
ja.lifeSummary: María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcayaが1158年にスペインで生まれた時，父VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayalaは30歳，母Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendozaは28歳であった。彼女はConde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretañaとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya(이)가 1158년 스페인에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya nasceu em 1158, em Espanha, seu pai, VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza, tinha 28 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya родилась в 1158 г. в местности Испания, её отцу по имени VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza, 28. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando María Sáenz de Salcedo, Señora de Ayala Linaje de Ayala, descendiente de las Casas Reales de Navarra, de Aragón y de León y de los Señores de Vizcaya nació en 1158, en España, su papá, VI señor Sancho García de Salcedo de Ayala, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Maria Íñiguez De Piedrola y Mendoza, tenía 28 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Conde Pedro Vélez, VII Señor de Guevara Linaje de Vela, Conde de Alava, descendiente de la Casa Real de Navarra, de los Duques de Vasconia, de Aquitana y de Bretaña. Murió en su pueblo.
